questions.question_text: We often 'marvel' at ancient structures that were built without modern tools. What does 'marvel' mean?

options.text: To feel bored and unimpressed by something ordinary
To feel wonder and amazement at something extraordinary
To study and analyse something from a scientific perspective
To fear and feel anxious about something unusual

questions.explanation_ar: marvel تعني يُبهر ويُدهش، أي الشعور بالإعجاب والدهشة أمام شيء مثير للانتباه بشكل استثنائي.

questions.question_text: Ancient construction methods continue to 'baffle' modern engineers. What does 'baffle' mean?

options.text: To clearly explain and make something easy to understand
To inspire and motivate someone to work harder
To completely confuse and puzzle someone beyond their understanding
To bore and fail to interest someone in a subject

questions.explanation_ar: baffle تعني يُحيّر ويُربك تماماً، أي أن شيئاً ما لا يمكن تفسيره أو فهمه مما يُصيب العقل بالحيرة.

questions.question_text: The Great Pyramid was one of history's most 'ambitious' construction projects. What does 'ambitious' mean?

options.text: Small in scale and completed very quickly with minimal effort
Requiring a high level of effort and aiming for a difficult goal
Simple in design and easy to complete using basic techniques
Unnecessary and lacking any clear practical purpose

questions.explanation_ar: ambitious تعني طموح وكبير الحجم، أي مشروع يستهدف هدفاً صعب التحقيق ويتطلب جهداً استثنائياً.

questions.question_text: The Great Pyramid is a 'colossal' monument containing 2.3 million stone blocks. What does 'colossal' mean?

options.text: Small and compact, designed to fit into a limited space
Extremely large and impressive in scale and size
Beautifully decorated with intricate patterns and colours
Fragile and easily damaged by weather or natural forces

questions.explanation_ar: colossal تعني ضخم وشاسع الحجم بشكل مبهر، وتصف الهياكل التي تتجاوز المقاييس المعتادة بكثير.

questions.question_text: What truly 'astounds' archaeologists is the pyramid's precise alignment with cardinal directions. What does 'astounds' mean?

options.text: Slightly interests without causing any strong reaction
Deeply shocks and amazes someone with surprise or wonder
Gradually convinces someone of an important fact over time
Mildly disappoints because expectations were not fully met

questions.explanation_ar: astounds تعني يُذهل ويُبهر بشكل عميق، أي أن الأمر يُحدث صدمة دهشة شديدة لا يمكن تجاهلها.

questions.question_text: When ancient engineers had to 'align' structures with cardinal directions, they needed to position them to face north, south, east, and west precisely.
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. align تعني يُوازي ويُوجّه بدقة، وتحديد الاتجاهات الأصلية (الشمال والجنوب والشرق والغرب) بدقة هي جوهر عملية التوجيه الهندسي.

questions.question_text: When construction methods 'perplex' scientists, the scientists have found clear and simple answers to all their questions.
questions.explanation_ar: خطأ. perplex تعني يُحيّر ويُعقّد، أي أن العلماء لا يزالون عاجزين عن الإجابة — وهو عكس إيجاد إجابات واضحة وبسيطة.

questions.question_text: Roman engineers had to _____ creative solutions to build the Pantheon's dome without internal support columns.
questions.explanation_ar: devise تعني يبتكر ويُصمّم حلاً جديداً، أي الوصول إلى أسلوب مبتكر لمواجهة تحدٍّ هندسي غير مسبوق.

questions.question_text: Which sentence uses 'used to' correctly to describe a past habit or state?

options.text: Ancient workers are used to carry stone blocks without machines.
Ancient workers used to carry stone blocks without machines.
Ancient workers used carry stone blocks without machines.
Ancient workers use to carry stone blocks without machines.

questions.explanation_ar: used to + مصدر يُعبّر عن عادة أو حالة ماضية لم تعد قائمة الآن. الخيار b صحيح بالتركيب الكامل: used to + carry.

questions.question_text: Complete: 'Builders _____ work without any modern machinery, but today technology does most of the heavy work.'

options.text: are used to
would
used to
use to

questions.explanation_ar: used to يُستخدم للتعبير عن وضع أو عادة ماضية لم تعد موجودة الآن. التركيب الصحيح هو used to + مصدر، وليس are used to أو use to.

questions.question_text: Which sentence uses 'used to' to describe a past STATE (not a repeated action)?

options.text: Workers used to carry heavy blocks every day.
Builders used to mix cement by hand each morning.
The Roman Empire used to be the most powerful empire in the world.
Engineers used to visit the construction site regularly.

questions.explanation_ar: used to يُستخدم لوصف حالات ماضية ثابتة (states) كالقوة والملكية، وليس فقط للأفعال المتكررة. الخيار c يصف حالة (being powerful) لا فعلاً متكرراً.

questions.question_text: Which sentence CANNOT use 'used to' (only simple past is correct)?

options.text: The engineer _____ work on the pyramid for twenty years.
People _____ believe the pyramid was built by slaves.
The workers _____ finish the pyramid in 2560 BCE.
Romans _____ build roads across their entire empire.

questions.explanation_ar: لا نستخدم used to مع أحداث وقعت مرة واحدة في وقت محدد. إتمام الهرم كان حدثاً لمرة واحدة عام 2560 ق.م، فالصحيح: finished.

questions.question_text: 'Ancient civilizations used to construct remarkable monuments without power tools or computers' uses 'used to' correctly.
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. used to construct يُعبّر عن ممارسة ماضية متكررة (بناء المنشآت) لا تحدث بنفس الطريقة اليوم.

questions.question_text: Match each 'used to' sentence to its correct meaning or use.

left_items: Workers used to transport stone using wooden sledges.
The pyramid used to be covered in polished white limestone.
Engineers never used to have computers to assist their calculations.
People used to think the pyramids were built by slaves.
right_items: a past belief that is now known to be incorrect
a past state or condition that no longer exists
a past repeated action or regular method
a past absence of something now considered normal

correct_answer.left: Workers used to transport stone using wooden sledges.
correct_answer.right: a past repeated action or regular method

correct_answer.left: The pyramid used to be covered in polished white limestone.
correct_answer.right: a past state or condition that no longer exists

correct_answer.left: Engineers never used to have computers to assist their calculations.
correct_answer.right: a past absence of something now considered normal

correct_answer.left: People used to think the pyramids were built by slaves.
correct_answer.right: a past belief that is now known to be incorrect

questions.explanation_ar: يُستخدم used to في أربعة سياقات: فعل متكرر في الماضي، حالة ماضية تغيّرت، غياب شيء أصبح شائعاً، ومعتقد ماضٍ أُثبت خطؤه.

questions.question_text: According to Passage A, approximately when was the Great Pyramid of Giza constructed?

options.text: Around 1200 BCE
Around 2580 BCE
Around 500 CE
Around 3500 BCE

questions.explanation_ar: ذكر النص أن هرم الجيزة الأكبر بُني حوالي عام 2580 قبل الميلاد، وهو يُعدّ أحد أكثر المشاريع طموحاً في التاريخ.

questions.question_text: According to Passage A, how precise was the alignment of the Great Pyramid's sides with the cardinal directions?

options.text: Within 1 full degree
Within half a degree
Within 3/60th of a degree
Within 5 full degrees

questions.explanation_ar: ذكر النص أن الهرم مُوجَّه نحو الاتجاهات الأصلية بدقة تصل إلى 3/60 من الدرجة — وهي دقة مذهلة تُحدّي قدرات المهندسين المعاصرين.

questions.question_text: Passage A says the Roman Pantheon's dome spans 43 meters 'without any internal support.' What does this engineering achievement suggest?

options.text: Roman engineers copied this technique from the ancient Egyptians
The building collapsed soon after construction was completed
Roman engineers mastered concrete construction at an extraordinary level
The dome is made entirely from wood rather than concrete

questions.explanation_ar: يُشير بناء قبة واسعة بدون أعمدة داخلية إلى إتقان رومي رائع لتقنيات الخرسانة وتوزيع الأحمال الهندسية.

questions.question_text: According to Passage A, the Great Pyramid's construction required an estimated 20,000 workers over two decades.
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. ذكر النص أن بناء الهرم استلزم ما يُقدَّر بـ 20,000 عامل على مدى عقدين من الزمن، مما يدل على المهارة التنظيمية الهائلة للمصريين القدماء.

questions.question_text: According to Passage A, the Great Pyramid of Giza was constructed around the year _____ BCE.
questions.explanation_ar: ذكر النص أن الهرم الأكبر بُني حوالي عام 2580 قبل الميلاد، ويُعدّ من أكثر المشاريع الهندسية التاريخية طموحاً وإثارة للإعجاب.

questions.question_text: Match each ancient structure or innovation from Passage A to its key engineering fact.

right_items: positioned to within 3/60th of a degree of cardinal directions
spans 43 meters with no internal support columns
a material that could set underwater and last for centuries
contains 2.3 million stone blocks weighing 2.5-15 tons each

correct_answer.right: contains 2.3 million stone blocks weighing 2.5-15 tons each
a material that could set underwater and last for centuries
spans 43 meters with no internal support columns
positioned to within 3/60th of a degree of cardinal directions

questions.explanation_ar: وردت هذه الحقائق الهندسية في النص مع مصادرها: الهرم (الكتل)، الخرسانة الرومانية (المتانة)، قبة البانثيون (الاتساع)، والتوجيه الهندسي (الدقة).
